meeting_notes: Contract sign-off target date (draft terms still unresolved)
email_summary: Strauss and Lowlands Dairy Co-op have been negotiating a contract renewal since January 2026, following expiry of the prior contract (STR-DAIRY-2024-007) on December 31, 2024. Deliveries have continued informally on expired terms through Q1 and Q2 2026, with confirmed shipments of 7 MT in January, 7 MT in February, and 20 MT scheduled across Q2. Key unresolved items are the minimum volume floor (Lowlands at 23 MT vs. Strauss at 22 MT) and base price (Lowlands at €3,270/ton vs. Strauss at €3,260/ton), and the Letter of Credit threshold arrangement has not yet received a final response from Lowlands' finance committee.

contract_terms.volume_min: 20 MT/quarter (expired contract; disputed in renewal — Lowlands proposes 23 MT, Strauss proposes 22 MT)
contract_terms.volume_max: 35 MT/quarter (expired contract; both parties agree 40 MT/quarter in draft renewal)
contract_terms.key_penalty: Late delivery beyond 3 business days: 1% of shipment value per day delayed, capped at 8%.

open_issues.description: The prior contract STR-DAIRY-2024-007 expired December 31, 2024, and no signed replacement exists. Strauss has been operating on informal month-to-month deliveries at expired terms since January 2026, creating legal and supply risk.
open_issues.source_ref: Contract STR-DAIRY-2024-007 (expired); DRAFT STR-DAIRY-2026-003 (not executed); email 2026-03-05 from David Cohen

open_issues.description: Deliveries since January 2026 are being processed at the expired contract price of €3,200/ton while both parties are negotiating a new price currently ranging from €3,260 to €3,270/ton, with no written amendment covering the interim period.
open_issues.source_ref: Contract STR-DAIRY-2024-007 (€3,200/ton); emails 2026-05-28 (Pieter: €3,270) and 2026-06-10 (David: €3,260); delivery emails from Anja Dekker

open_issues.description: Minimum volume floor remains unresolved: Lowlands' firm position is 23 MT/quarter (board-approved), Strauss's latest final position is 22 MT/quarter. A one-MT gap is blocking contract execution.
open_issues.source_ref: Email 2026-05-28 from Pieter van den Berg; email 2026-06-10 from David Cohen

open_issues.description: Base price remains unresolved: Lowlands is at €3,270/ton and Strauss is at €3,260/ton, a €10/ton gap that has not been bridged despite multiple rounds of negotiation.
open_issues.source_ref: Email 2026-05-28 from Pieter van den Berg; email 2026-06-10 from David Cohen; DRAFT STR-DAIRY-2026-003 Notes

open_issues.description: Strauss proposed that Letter of Credit terms apply only to shipments above 25 MT/quarter, with open account below that threshold. Lowlands acknowledged the proposal as 'reasonable' and said they would take it to the finance committee, but no final decision has been communicated.
open_issues.source_ref: Email 2026-02-10 from David Cohen; email 2026-02-17 from Pieter van den Berg; email 2026-06-10 from David Cohen

commodity_benchmark: The dairy commodity index has declined from approximately 17.08 in late April 2026 to around 16.19 by late June 2026, a drop of roughly 5% over the past two months, suggesting softening market prices that give Strauss modest leverage to resist Lowlands' proposed price increase above the expired €3,200/ton base.
heads_up: There is no signed contract in place — Strauss has been operating without legal cover since January 2026 — and the two remaining blockers (€10/ton price gap and 1 MT volume floor gap) must be resolved simultaneously with the outstanding Letter of Credit finance committee response before any document can be executed. Pieter's May 28 email flagged that informal operation 'cannot continue indefinitely,' signalling Lowlands may escalate if a deal is not closed soon.
negotiation_brief: Strauss has no active signed contract with Lowlands Dairy and has been exposed since January 2026, which weakens its leverage in any dispute but also means Lowlands has equal incentive to close. The supplier is quoting €3,270/ton, a 2.2% premium over Strauss's position of €3,260 and a 5.3% increase over the expired €3,200/ton base; however, the dairy commodity index has fallen roughly 5% since late April, which supports Strauss's resistance to a significant price increase and could justify pushing Lowlands to meet at €3,260. Walking in, the single most important dynamic is that both gaps are narrow — €10/ton on price and 1 MT on volume minimum — and Lowlands has signalled they cannot go below 23 MT (board vote); Strauss should consider whether accepting 23 MT in exchange for locking in €3,260 is the right trade, and must also get a definitive answer on the LC threshold proposal before signing.

financial_signals.delivery_reliability_score: Good — all shipments on time: January shipment of 7 MT dispatched January 31 with ETA February 6 confirmed; Q2 schedule of 20 MT across three dates accepted without delay mention.

benchmark.unit_label: USD per hundredweight (CME Class III Milk)
benchmark.source: CME Class III Milk Futures (DC=F) via Yahoo Finance
benchmark.note: CME dairy futures are thinly traded and the series may be incomplete. Treat as directional indicator only, not a precise benchmark for direct negotiation use.

raw_emails.subject: Contract renewal — Lowlands Dairy
raw_emails.body:
Hi Pieter,

Happy new year. As you know our contract with Lowlands expired December 31. We're operating month-to-month for now, but I'd like to get the renewal discussion started properly. Can we schedule a call this month?

David

raw_emails.subject: RE: Contract renewal — Lowlands Dairy
raw_emails.body:
David,

Good to hear from you, and happy new year. Yes, renewal is on our radar. Before we go to formal terms I need to flag that our production planning team has been reviewing outbound commitments and we expect changes on the volume side. Happy to discuss on a call — how does January 22 look?

One thing to note before we get into terms: please be aware that all Lowlands Dairy pricing is denominated in EUR. Strauss is responsible for any currency conversion costs; the applicable rate is that of the payment date, not the order date. We have had confusion on this with other customers so I want to be explicit from the start.

Pieter

raw_emails.subject: Renewal framework — Lowlands Dairy proposal
raw_emails.body:
David,

Good call earlier. As discussed, here is our proposed framework for the 2026 contract:

- Term: 18 months
- Volume: minimum 25 MT/quarter, maximum 40 MT/quarter
- Price: €3,350/ton
- Payment: Net 30

The key change from the old contract is the minimum commitment — we have moved it to 25 MT from 20 MT. This reflects new production scheduling requirements. We cannot hold capacity for a customer at 20 MT minimum anymore.

I have also been instructed by our finance committee to raise two commercial points for the renewal:

First, for the renewed contract volumes, our finance committee requires payment by irrevocable Letter of Credit denominated in EUR, issued by a correspondent bank acceptable to our co-op, no later than 30 days before each shipment date. We can discuss whether this applies to all shipments or only those above a threshold volume.

Second, we would like to move to net 60 payment terms for the renewal. Current net 30 is difficult for our co-op cash flow given the 4-6 week cross-border SEPA transfer timelines on the Israel route. This is something we would like to raise as a negotiation point, not a demand.

I have cc'd Anja Dekker, our volume planning lead.

Pieter

raw_emails.subject: RE: Renewal framework — Lowlands Dairy proposal
raw_emails.body:
Pieter,

Thanks for the framework. A few reactions:

On price — €3,350 is a significant step up from the old contract. Can you share what is driving that number?

On minimum volume — 25 MT vs 20 MT is something I need to check internally. Our Q4 2025 actual offtake was around 19 MT so committing to a 25 MT floor is not straightforward.

On the Letter of Credit — we have never operated on LC terms with Lowlands. This adds administrative burden and cost on our side. I will need to understand what is driving the finance committee's position before I can respond.

On net 60 — we operate on net 30 with most suppliers. I understand the cash flow argument but this would be a change we would need to evaluate carefully.

I'll come back with a counter by end of next week.

David

raw_emails.subject: Q1 delivery — January/February
raw_emails.body:
David,

This is Anja from planning. While renewal is being discussed we are continuing to process your monthly orders under the old terms. The January shipment of 7 MT left January 31, ETA at your site February 6.

For February we have you down for 7 MT around February 20 — please confirm this is still needed.

Anja

raw_emails.subject: RE: Q1 delivery — confirmed
raw_emails.body:
Confirmed, February 7 MT on the 20th is fine. Thanks Anja.

David

raw_emails.subject: Counter-proposal — renewal terms
raw_emails.body:
Pieter,

Our counter on the renewal framework:

- Term: 18 months (agreed)
- Volume: minimum 20 MT/quarter, maximum 40 MT/quarter (we need to hold the original minimum)
- Price: €3,250/ton
- Payment: Net 30 (we cannot move to net 60 — our AP processes are built around 30-day cycles and this would require a policy exception I am unlikely to get approved)

On the Letter of Credit — we would propose to maintain open account terms for shipments under 25 MT/quarter, with LC applicable only for shipments above that threshold. This limits the administrative burden while addressing your finance committee's concern for larger volumes.

David

raw_emails.subject: RE: Counter-proposal
raw_emails.body:
David,

Thank you for the counter. On price — we could consider €3,280 but not lower. Input costs simply do not allow it.

On minimum volume — 20 MT is not workable. The co-op voted on this. We could consider 23 MT as a compromise.

On the LC — your proposal on the threshold is reasonable. We can take that back to the finance committee.

On net 60 — we accept that you cannot move to 60 days. We will drop this request.

Still some gap to close. Can we get on a call this week?

Pieter

raw_emails.subject: Following up — renewal status
raw_emails.body:
Pieter,

We have been going back and forth since January and are still not aligned on price and volume minimum. I want to make sure we don't drift further on an informal basis without a signed contract.

Where do things stand? Are you still at 23 MT minimum / €3,280?

David

raw_emails.subject: RE: Renewal status
raw_emails.body:
David,

Yes, we are still at those positions. I have had our legal team put together a draft contract document — sending separately. We would like to have something signed before end of April.

Pieter

raw_emails.subject: Draft contract — open items
raw_emails.body:
Pieter,

We have reviewed the draft. The main issues remain: minimum volume (we reviewed internally and 20 MT is our limit) and the price (we can go to €3,260 but not €3,280).

I know we have been at an impasse on these for a while. Is there any flexibility on your side, or do you need to go back to the co-op?

David

raw_emails.subject: Q2 delivery schedule
raw_emails.body:
David,

Sending Q2 delivery schedule as we continue on the informal basis:
- April 28: 7 MT
- May 19: 7 MT
- June 10: 6 MT
Total Q2: 20 MT. Please confirm if these dates work.

Note from Pieter: renewal discussion still ongoing, he will follow up separately.

Anja

raw_emails.subject: Renewal — still open
raw_emails.body:
David,

I owe you an update. I brought the 20 MT minimum back to our board and they will not approve it. The co-op's position is firm at 23 MT. On price we could meet at €3,270.

I know this is frustrating. We have been very patient operating without a signed contract but this cannot continue indefinitely. What is Strauss's final position?

Pieter

raw_emails.subject: RE: Renewal — Strauss position
raw_emails.body:
Pieter,

I have escalated internally. Here is where we land: we can move to 22 MT minimum as a compromise and €3,270 on price. That is our final position.

On the LC — can you confirm the finance committee's decision on our threshold proposal (open account below 25 MT, LC above)? That needs to be resolved before we can sign.

David

raw_contracts.supplier_03_lowlands_dairy_DRAFT.md:
# Contract Summary — Lowlands Dairy Co-op (DRAFT — NOT EXECUTED)

**Contract ID:** STR-DAIRY-2026-003
**Supplier:** Lowlands Dairy Co-op (Netherlands)
**Category:** Dairy — whole milk powder
**Status:** DRAFT — UNDER NEGOTIATION AS OF JUNE 2026. THIS DOCUMENT HAS NOT BEEN SIGNED BY EITHER PARTY AND IS NOT LEGALLY BINDING.
**Proposed Effective Date:** TBD (pending agreement)
**Proposed Term:** 18 months

## Payment Terms
Net 30 from invoice date (agreed by both parties).

## Volume Commitment
**OPEN ITEM — NOT AGREED:**
- Lowlands proposes: minimum 25 MT/quarter (later moved to 23 MT as compromise), maximum 40 MT/quarter
- Strauss position: minimum 20 MT/quarter, maximum 40 MT/quarter
- Maximum 40 MT/quarter: agreed by both parties

## Pricing
**OPEN ITEM — NOT AGREED:**
- Lowlands proposes: €3,350/ton
- Strauss counter: €3,260/ton
- Last known positions (as of May 2026): Lowlands at €3,270/ton, Strauss at €3,260/ton — gap of €10/ton remains open

## Penalty Clauses
Proposed (not yet finalized):
- Late delivery beyond 3 business days: 1% of shipment value per day, capped at 8%.
- Quality failure: full replacement at supplier cost.

## Notes
DRAFT STATUS — Do not treat as an executed contract. Two items remain open: (1) minimum volume floor (Lowlands: 23 MT, Strauss: 20 MT), (2) base price (Lowlands: €3,270, Strauss: €3,260). The email thread contains the most current negotiating positions. The prototype should explicitly flag that no valid active contract exists for this supplier — Strauss is currently operating without a signed agreement, creating legal and supply risk. Reference the OLD contract (STR-DAIRY-2024-007) for expired terms being used informally in the interim.
raw_contracts.supplier_03_lowlands_dairy_OLD.md:
# Contract Summary — Lowlands Dairy Co-op (EXPIRED)

**Contract ID:** STR-DAIRY-2024-007
**Supplier:** Lowlands Dairy Co-op (Netherlands)
**Category:** Dairy — whole milk powder
**Effective Date:** 2024-01-01
**Term:** 12 months
**Expiry Date:** 2024-12-31 — **CONTRACT EXPIRED. NOT CURRENTLY IN FORCE.**

## Payment Terms
Net 30 from invoice date.

## Volume Commitment
Minimum 20 tons/quarter, maximum 35 tons/quarter. Volumes below minimum for two consecutive quarters trigger a contract review.

## Pricing
€3,200/ton, fixed for the 12-month contract term.

## Penalty Clauses
- Late delivery beyond 3 business days: 1% of shipment value per day delayed, capped at 8%.
- Quality failure (below specification on independent inspection): full replacement at supplier cost, no charge to Strauss.

## Notes
This contract expired December 31, 2024. A renewal has been under negotiation since January 2026 but has not been executed (see DRAFT file). Strauss and Lowlands have been operating informally on month-to-month deliveries at the expired contract terms pending agreement. The key commercial dispute in the email thread involves the minimum volume commitment: this expired contract specifies 20 MT/quarter minimum; Lowlands is proposing 23-25 MT/quarter in renewal negotiations. The prototype should flag that no active signed contract exists and surface this as a compliance/legal exposure.
